MORE GRIEF :
FOR The c: n.

pages.raw_text:
Failure of the Great Northern
railway to supply the Penrith Wood
Co, with cars, when the same were
ordered, may give the big corpora
tion a little taste of the same medi-
cine that is oftimcs dealt out in CO.
pious doses to its helpless custom
ers. Under the provisions OF the
reciprocal demurrage charges, which
was passed for the protection of the
shippers after railroads had begun
the practice of imposing demurrage
charges for delay in unloading cars,
the Penrith Wood Co. has instituted
proceedings in the superior court,
asking damages in the sum of $1,000.
This sum amounts to 81 per car per
day during which the ordered deliV-
cry of cars was delayed by the rail-
road company.


The complaint, which was filed
this morning. alleges that the Great
Northern Railway company gave no
heed to the plaintiff company's re
quest for cars and failed to deliver
them to it's service within the time
specified by law.
